title: Add release automation + Homebrew parity for shape (match rvl)
description:
Implementation Scope:
Bring `shape` release automation to parity with `rvl` so releases are reproducible and publishable without manual steps.

Deliverables:
- Add a dedicated release workflow (build matrix for Linux/macOS/Windows targets).
- Package and publish release artifacts (tar.gz/zip) to GitHub Releases.
- Generate and publish SHA256SUMS, cosign signatures/cert, SBOM, and provenance artifact.
- Add automated Homebrew tap formula update for `shape` (parallel to `rvl` tap update flow).
- Ensure install scripts (`scripts/install.sh`, `scripts/install.ps1`) resolve latest release correctly for `shape`.

Definition of Done:
- Bumping `Cargo.toml` version on `main` triggers release automation end-to-end.
- A new tag/release is created (or updated) with all expected artifacts.
- Homebrew tap formula for `shape` updates to new version + checksums.
- README install claims match actual release outputs and CI behavior.

Validation:
- Dry run or staging run of workflow demonstrates matrix build success.
- Verify release assets exist and checksums/cosign verification succeed.
- Verify `brew install cmdrvl/tap/shape` installs the just-released version.
close_reason: Implemented release workflow, Homebrew update automation, and install scripts for shape

This is fail-open behavior in integrity verification. Fix by making missing cosign artifacts a hard failure when cosign is present (unless SHAPE_NO_VERIFY=1).
close_reason: Covered by current install.sh behavior: cosign asset downloads are mandatory under set -e when cosign is installed

title: Implement CLI args, flag validation, and deferred flag shape
description:
Implementation Scope:
Implement clap parsing for positional args and flags in PLAN v0.1, including current flags (`--key`, `--delimiter`, `--json`, `--describe`, `--version`) and deferred-shape flags (`--profile`, `--profile-id`, `--lock`, `--max-rows`, `--max-bytes`) with correct validation semantics.

Deliverables:
- Args parser with mutual exclusivity for `--profile` and `--profile-id`.
- Correct command behavior when `--describe` is passed without positional files.
- Validation errors mapped to refusal/CLI error behavior (exit 2).

Definition of Done:
- CLI parser accepts valid combinations and rejects invalid ones.
- Deferred flags are represented in args contracts for schema stability.
- Parsing behavior matches PLAN wording and expected precedence.

Test Cases:
- Parse success tests for happy-path invocations.
- Parse failure tests for ambiguous profile selectors and malformed limits.
- `--describe` no-arg invocation test.
acceptance_criteria: 1) CLI parser accepts all required v0.1 flags and positional arguments.\n2) Invalid flag combinations, including profile selector ambiguity, fail deterministically.\n3) shape --describe succeeds without positional file arguments.
close_reason: Implemented clap args + validation tests
